channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Graham Platner faces calls to leave Maine Senate race after sexual assault allegation
item.description: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Democratic strategist on calls for Graham Platner to leave Maine Senate race
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic strategist Joel Payne about high-profile Democratic calls for Graham Platner to withdraw from the Maine Senate race.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic strategist Joel Payne about high-profile Democratic calls for Graham Platner to withdraw from the Maine Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Ukraine to press NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after latest Russian attacks
item.description: Ukraine pushes allies in NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after the latest major Russian attacks kill more than 20 in Kyiv and surrounding area.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine pushes allies in NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after the latest major Russian attacks kill more than 20 in Kyiv and surrounding area.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Graham Platner faces calls to end his Senate bid after sexual assault allegation, NATO summit begins in Turkey, U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with loss to Belgium.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner faces calls to end his Senate bid after sexual assault allegation, NATO summit begins in Turkey, U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with loss to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Gov. Sherrill ran on freezing electricity rates. Can she bring relief to New Jersey?
item.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill, a Democrat, campaigned on freezing electricity rates in New Jersey. Six months into her term, can her campaign promises bring relief to the high cost of power in the Garden State?
item.content:encoded: Gov. Mikie Sherrill, a Democrat, campaigned on freezing electricity rates in New Jersey. Six months into her term, can her campaign promises bring relief to the high cost of power in the Garden State?
item.dc:creator: Michael Hayes

item.title: How a tiny fleck of gold changed American history
item.description: The Gold Rush lured hundreds of thousands of people to California and changed American history. It all started with a tiny gold flake.
item.content:encoded: The Gold Rush lured hundreds of thousands of people to California and changed American history. It all started with a tiny gold flake.
item.dc:creator: Clare Lombardo

item.title: Pastor Doug Wilson on why he believes the U.S. should be a Christian theocracy
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Pastor Doug Wilson, who recently gave a sermon at the Pentagon, why he wants the U.S. to become a Christian theocracy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Preliminary hearing begins for the man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk
item.description: A hearing for the man accused of assassinating conservative influencer Charlie Kirk has begun. The prosecution aims to show it has enough evidence for charges that could result in the death penalty.
item.content:encoded: A hearing for the man accused of assassinating conservative influencer Charlie Kirk has begun. The prosecution aims to show it has enough evidence for charges that could result in the death penalty.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Ballard

item.title: Egypt takes on defending champs Argentina on Tuesday
item.description: Egypt takes on World Cup defending champion Argentina Tuesday. Their success in making it to the round of 16 has electrified fans throughout the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: Egypt takes on World Cup defending champion Argentina Tuesday. Their success in making it to the round of 16 has electrified fans throughout the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Gen Z protesters in India demand political reform and accountability
item.description: Indian Gen Z protesters in Delhi, calling themselves "cockroaches" after a judge's insult, demand political and job reforms from Modi's government amid rising youth unemployment.
item.content:encoded: Indian Gen Z protesters in Delhi, calling themselves "cockroaches" after a judge's insult, demand political and job reforms from Modi's government amid rising youth unemployment.
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar
